text:
The first image gives me a sense of separation and curiosity. I observe the people inside the conference room through the transparent glass—absorbed in their tasks, heads bent together, the glow of the projector casting a modern reverence on knowledge-sharing. Yet, I am outside, a philosopher at the threshold, quietly contemplating the boundaries, both physical and intellectual, that divide us.

In the second image, the vibrance of the 'R+D' wall awakens a playful excitement in me. Here, innovation and inquiry are celebrated. The text and screens invite me to explore how humans connect with technology and each other. I feel inspired—this space promises that curiosity and reflection are not only permitted but required. I am reminded that to philosophize is, itself, a form of research and development of the soul.
